blockingIssues.description: LocalDir staging does not fully satisfy `VAL-SANDBOX-004`: symlinked ancestors are not rejected and the current revalidation still leaves a TOCTOU swap window before `read_dir`/`fs::copy`, so unsafe host content can still be ingested.

Unix-local shell and PTY execution are not actually confined to the sandbox workspace: `validate_shell_command()` only token-scans the command string before `/bin/sh -lc` executes it on the host, so embedded interpreters or shell expansions can still persist writes outside the workspace.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that macOS PTY work in this mission environment can rely on `/usr/bin/script` as the local PTY primitive.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `rust-sandbox-worker` to explicitly require adversarial confinement regressions for sandbox-safety work, including symlink ancestry and TOCTOU path-swap cases when a contract assertion is about workspace or LocalDir confinement.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `sandbox-localdir-and-workspace-safety` review found that the landed regression was named for "symlinked or swapped sources" but only exercised a static child symlink, which left the contract's ancestor-symlink and live-swap cases uncovered.
